overall.description: A ship simulator game showing nighttime navigation from the bridge. The vessel moves steadily through calm waters with navigation displays visible overhead, approaching a coastal lighthouse.

agents.ego_description: operating the ship's navigation systems and steering through open coastal waters at night

entered.detail: triangular bow structure of the vessel with metal railings and deck plates
metal safety railing running along the left side of the bow
metal safety railing running along the right side of the bow
dark calm water extending to the horizon

segments.scene_description: Navigating from the enclosed ship's bridge at night, viewing the bow cutting through calm dark waters. The radar display rotates continuously overhead while the navigation chart shows the vessel's position along the coastline.

events.description: coastal lighthouse structure becomes visible on the starboard horizon

agents.ego_description: maintaining course while monitoring the approaching lighthouse on the starboard side

entered.detail: tall white coastal lighthouse structure with illuminated top

segments.scene_description: Continuing the coastal approach, the lighthouse becomes prominently visible on the starboard side and gradually grows larger as the vessel advances. The ship maintains steady navigation through the calm night waters toward the coastal marker.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot follows a continuous forward movement from the enclosed bridge of a ship as it navigates through a dark night toward a distant coastal beacon. It begins with a view of the bow cutting through calm, dark waters, while a rotating radar display and navigation chart are visible overhead. As the vessel maintains its steady course along the coastline, the perspective shifts toward the starboard side where a lighthouse emerges from the darkness. The scene then expands as the ship advances, causing the lighthouse to grow prominently larger and more detailed against the night sky. Finally, the camera continues its steady approach, locking onto the coastal marker as the vessel glides through the still water.
